channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Middle East latest: Houthi rebels fire missile at Israel hours after airstrikes on Yemen airport
item.description: Houthi rebels in Yemen fired a missile at Israel early Friday, hours after a wave of Israeli airstrikes hit Yemen's main airport as the World Health Organization’s director-general was about to board a flight there.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea's opposition-controlled National Assembly votes to impeach acting President Han
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s opposition-controlled National Assembly voted Friday to impeach acting President Han Duck-soo despite vehement protests by governing party lawmakers, further deepening the country’s political crisis set off by President Yoon Suk Yeol’s stunning imposition of m
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran regime under 'immense pressure' amid incoming Trump admin policies, regional losses, economic woes
item.description: As a result of the Iranian regime’s financing of Islamist terrorist organization and its economic corruption, the people of the Mideast nation are facing widespread gas and power outages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Croatia's outspoken president is seeking reelection. He faces several contenders in Sunday's vote
item.description: Croatia’s left-leaning president, who has been critical of Western military support for Ukraine in the war with Russia, is running for reelection this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Saša Kavić, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ukraine's military intelligence says North Korean troops are suffering heavy battlefield losses
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — North Korean troops are suffering heavy losses in the fighting in Russia's Kursk region and facing logistical difficulties as a result of Ukrainian attacks, Ukraine’s military intelligence said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: India's former prime minister Manmohan Singh, architect of economic reforms, dies at 92
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — India’s former Prime Minister Manmohan Singh, widely regarded as the architect of India’s economic reform program and a landmark nuclear deal with the United States, has died. He was 92.
item.dc:creator: AIJAZ HUSSAIN and ASHOK SHARMA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli attorney general orders probe into report that alleged Netanyahu's wife harassed opponents
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel’s attorney general has ordered police to open an investigation into Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s wife on suspicion of harassing political opponents and a witness in the Israeli leader’s corruption trial.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: WHO chief says he was at Yemen airport as Israeli bombs fell nearby
item.description: A wave of Israeli airstrikes hit Yemen's main airport Thursday just as the World Health Organization’s director-general said he was about to board a flight there. One of the U.N. plane’s crew was wounded, WHO chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said in a post on X.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia cautions against 'hypotheses' in Azerbaijan Airlines crash
item.description: Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov told reporters that it was "necessary to wait for the end of this probe" before casting any speculation.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden arrives in St. Croix for last vacation as president
item.description: President Biden on Thursday arrived in St. Croix for his last vacation as president before leaving office next month. The president left the White House the day after Christmas with first lady Jill Biden. He arrived in St. Croix in the U.S. Virgin Islands around 2 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Musk, Ramaswamy defend Silicon Valley's foreign-born hires
item.description: Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy, co-chairs of President-elect Trump’s new “Department of Government Efficiency,” are defending the tech industry’s reliance on foreign-born engineers as the incoming Trump administration prepares to crack down on immigration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: The US says it pushed retraction of a famine warning for north Gaza. Aid groups express concern
item.description: U.S. officials say they asked for — and got — the retraction of an independent monitor's warning of imminent famine in north Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: More than 10,000 migrants died this year trying to reach Spain by sea, aid group says
item.description: MADRID (AP) — More than 10,000 migrants died while trying to reach Spain by sea this year, a report released by a Spanish migration rights group said on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia arrests 4 suspects accused of plotting to kill top military officers on Ukraine's orders
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russia's top security agency said Thursday that it has arrested several suspects accused of involvement in an alleged Ukrainian plot to assassinate senior military officers, an announcement that follows the killing of a top Russian general last week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel strikes Houthi rebels in Yemen's capital while the WHO chief says he was meters away
item.description: A new round of Israeli airstrikes in Yemen has targeted the Houthi rebel-held capital of Sanaa and multiple ports.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden condemns Russia's 'outrageous' Christmas attack on Ukraine
item.description: President Biden on Wednesday denounced Russia’s latest attack on Ukraine, declaring the wave of missiles and drones “outrageous.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taliban claims Pakistani airstrikes killed 46
item.description: A Taliban government official said the airstrikes Pakistan launched on Afghanistan Tuesday killed 46 people, including women and children, according to The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Pope calls for end to global wars, divisions in Christmas message
item.description: Pope Francis called for an end to global wars and divisiveness in his annual Christmas Day message, given Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Katie Wadington, The Hill

item.title: Netanyahu warns Houthis amid calls for Israel to wipe out terror leadership as it did with Nasrallah, Sinwar
item.description: Former Israeli officials discuss the possibility of Jerusalem targeting the Houthi leadership in the same way it took out other terror leaders from Hamas and Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump could face renewed ISIS threat in Syria as Turkey goes after US ally
item.description: Concerns are mounting that President-elect Trump could face an ISIS resurgence in Syria as Turkey-backed forces hit U.S.-allied SDF troops in a move that could divide the coalitions abilities to fight ISIS.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Matt Petgrave, involved in play that led to Adam Johnson's death, asks for donations for legal costs
item.description: Hockey player Matt Petgrave has called the death of Adam Johnson “a tragic accident” in a public appeal for donations to his legal costs amid a police investigation.
item.dc:creator: KEN MAGUIRE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Latvia claims to have displayed the world's first Christmas tree in 1510 adorned with artificial roses
item.description: Latvia claims to hold the title of the world's first Christmas tree, as does Estonia. Several sources cite that the first written record of a Christmas tree belongs to Latvia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China says US is 'playing with fire' after latest military aid for Taiwan
item.description: U.S. military sales and assistance aim to help Taiwan defend itself and deter China from launching an attack.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Santa Claus is en route in his flight across the world to deliver gifts
item.description: When Saint Nick starts his journey on Dec. 24, families can track Santa's sleigh using the NORAD Santa tracker.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Two US Navy pilots shot down over Red Sea in apparent 'friendly fire' incident: US military
item.description: Two U.S. Navy pilots were shot down Sunday over the Red Sea in what appeared to be "friendly fire", but were found alive, the U.S. military said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Germans mourn the 5 killed and 200 injured in the apparent attack on a Christmas market
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — Germans on Saturday mourned the victims of an apparent attack in which authorities say a doctor drove into a busy outdoor Christmas market, killing five people, injuring 200 others and shaking the public’s sense of security at what would otherwise be a time of joy.
item.dc:creator: KIRSTEN GRIESHABER, GEIR MOULSON and VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: In a calendar rarity, Hanukkah starts this year on Christmas Day
item.description: Hanukkah, Judaism’s eight-day Festival of Lights, begins this year on Christmas Day, which has only happened four times since 1900. For some rabbis, the intersection of the two religious holidays provides an auspicious occasion for interfaith engagement.
item.dc:creator: DAVID CRARY, Associated Press, NewsNation
